title: gladiolus communis var. byzantinus
intro: Gladiolus communis var. byzantinus, also known as the Byzantine gladiolus, is a perennial plant species within the genus Gladiolus. This plant is known for its vibrant purple flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The roots of the Byzantine gladiolus contain anthraquinones, which have been used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems and inflammation, due to their laxative and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions and decoctions that can be used to treat these health issues, and it is also sometimes used as a food ingredient in traditional cuisine.
